title: The Unholy Three

evidence.note: Published 1925; the 95-year US copyright term has expired for works published through 1930, so the film print is public domain in the US by term expiry.

diy_links.label: Stanford Copyright Renewal DB — search “The Unholy Three” (renewals ~1952, 1953, 1954)
Catalog of Copyright Entries (archive.org) — browse the Motion Pictures renewal-year volume by hand
US Copyright Office public records (1978+ renewals/assignments)

editorial: The Unholy Three is a 1925 American silent crime melodrama directed by Tod Browning for Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer, starring Lon Chaney as a sideshow ventriloquist who leads a trio of circus grifters into a robbery-and-murder spree. It cemented the Browning-Chaney partnership and was successful enough that Chaney remade it as his only sound film in 1930 (a separate, later work with its own rights). As a 1925 US publication, the film print is public domain by expiry of the 95-year US copyright term; other layers remain undetermined.
